question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file where `sidecar.getTypes()` contains a type ordinal that is present in `acceptedTypes` but the actual `TransactionSidecarRecord` entries have a different `sidecarRecordsCase`, causing the first-stage filter to pass but the second-stage `acceptedTypes.contains(getSidecarType(t))` filter to drop all records, silently suppressing all transactions in that sidecar?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename)` to throw a `TransientProviderException`, which is not caught inside the `getSidecar()` reactive chain, propagating as an error through `flatMap` in `downloadSidecars()` and causing the entire sidecar pipeline to fail, suppressing all transaction sidecar data for the record file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ArrayListMultimap::create` factory in `Multimaps.toMultimap(TransactionSidecarRecord::getConsensusTimestamp, Function.identity(), ArrayListMultimap::create)` to cause unbounded memory growth by crafting a sidecar with millions of unique `consensusTimestamp` values, exhausting heap memory and crashing the importer, preventing all transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSidecarType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionSidecarRecord` with a future/unknown `sidecarRecordsCase` enum value that is not handled by the `switch` statement in `getSidecarType()`, causing the `default` branch to invoke `Utility.handleRecoverableError()` and return `SIDECAR_TYPE_UNKNOWN_VALUE`, which is then filtered out by `acceptedTypes.contains()`, suppressing the transaction's sidecar data without any error being raised?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `filter(t -> acceptedTypes.isEmpty() || acceptedTypes.contains(getSidecarType(t)))` second-stage filter by crafting a sidecar where all `TransactionSidecarRecord` entries have unknown types, causing the filter to drop all records when `acceptedTypes` is non-empty, and the `records` Multimap to be empty, resulting in no sidecar data being set on any `RecordItem` and all transaction execution details being suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file with a valid hash but zero-length `bytes`, causing `sidecarFileReader.read(sidecar, streamFileData)` to produce a `SidecarFile` with an empty records list, so that `flatMapIterable(SidecarFile::getRecords)` emits nothing and all transactions in the record file that depend on this sidecar have their execution details permanently suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `recordFile.getItems().forEach(recordItem -> { var timestamp = recordItem.getTransactionRecord().getConsensusTimestamp(); if (records.containsKey(timestamp)) { recordItem.setSidecarRecords(records.get(timestamp)); } })` loop by crafting a sidecar with a timestamp that matches a high-value transaction but contains empty or malicious sidecar records, causing `setSidecarRecords()` to overwrite the legitimate sidecar data with attacker-controlled content?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the `Flux.fromIterable(recordFile.getSidecars())` source to emit sidecar entries in an order that causes the `flatMap` operator to process a poisoned sidecar first, throwing an exception that cancels the entire reactive pipeline before legitimate sidecars are processed, suppressing all transaction sidecar data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar filename (via `sidecar.getName()`) that, when passed to `StreamFilename.from(recordFilename, sidecar.getName())`, produces a `StreamFilename` with a `bucketFilePath` pointing to a different node's directory, causing the importer to download a sidecar from the wrong node, fail the hash check, and suppress all transactions associated with the legitimate sidecar?
